title: Italic / true-cursive styles for shipped Latin and script families
context: Italic and true-cursive styles expand the typographic utility of shipped families for body text, educational materials, and emphasis. This is a backlog/future item scheduled after upright styles are stable (post fonts-ship-104). Drawing italics is significant additional craft work; the accessibility-differentiation set must be re-applied to italic forms. Not started until uprights are stable and reviewed.
objective: Design and draw true italic styles for the shipped Latin accessibility family and any applicable script families, applying accessibility-critical glyph differentiation to italic forms, building cleanly through the pipeline, and shipping as part of an updated font release.
acceptanceCriteria: True italic (not slanted roman) styles drawn for the Latin accessibility family; italic forms maintain the accessibility differentiation set (I/l/1, O/0, b/d/p/q, rn/m).
UFO italic source + designspace commits cleanly; builds to static and variable binaries.
FontBakery Universal + GF profiles pass (no FAIL) for italic instances; WARNs triaged.
Variable font italic axis or separate italic family integrates correctly with existing upright.
provenance.json updated with italic glyph source records.
No proprietary tracing; clean-room or OFL/PD-derived italic sources only.
output: A pull request adding true italic styles to the shipped Latin (and applicable script) families, with updated UFO source, designspace, built binaries, and provenance.json.
